name: Bachelor of Science in Accountancy
college: College of Business Administration and Accountancy (CBAA)
overview: The BS Accountancy program is one of the most rigorous and prestigious business programs in the Philippines. It trains students in financial reporting, auditing, taxation, and management accounting. Graduates are prepared to take the CPA Licensure Examination and pursue careers in public accounting, corporate finance, and government auditing.
ideal_student: This program is ideal for students who are highly detail-oriented, disciplined, and comfortable working with numbers for extended periods. Students who value precision, integrity, and systematic thinking excel here. It suits those who want a clear professional path with a board examination as a milestone and strong job security in the finance sector.
skills: Financial accounting, auditing, taxation, cost accounting, business law, analytical reasoning, attention to detail, numerical accuracy, report preparation, ethical judgment, and time management.
careers: Certified Public Accountant (CPA), external auditor, internal auditor, tax consultant, financial analyst, budget officer, accounting manager, government auditor, CFO (with experience), finance professor.
work_environment: Graduates typically work in accounting firms, banks, corporations, government agencies, and NGOs. Work involves preparing financial statements, reviewing records, filing tax reports, and advising clients or management on financial matters. Peak seasons like tax filing periods are intensive.
strand_alignment: Strongly recommended for students from ABM strand with strong mathematics and business subjects performance. STEM students with high math performance may also qualify.
keywords: accounting numbers finance money audit tax CPA business financial statements bookkeeping budget corporation bank government precise detail-oriented math analytical reporting professional board exam licensure commerce economics ledger payroll investment
holland_rationale: BSA is primarily Conventional because it demands precision, systematic rule-following, structured data management, and adherence to established accounting standards and regulations. Enterprising is secondary because accountants often advise clients, manage financial decisions, and work within competitive professional environments.
